_comment: Query manifest for the search-impact benchmark (scripts/benchmark_search_impact.py), which measures whether stripping running headers/footers changes keyword (BM25/FTS5) ranking. Realistic relevance-labeled queries come from benchmark_data/ground_truth.json (attention, gpt3 — page-number-only boilerplate, so query terms never collide). This file adds GDPR queries, whose running header carries real words: `control` queries are ordinary content lookups (expected: no change), `collision` queries deliberately overlap the running header `Official Journal of the European Union` (expected: stripping removes header-driven noise pages). Distortion is measured label-free as top-K set change, so collision queries need no relevance labels.

gdpr.boilerplate: running header on every page: '<date> L 119/N Official Journal of the European Union EN'
